topic: Vintage Champagne selection and extended ageing

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A producer declares a Champagne from one selected harvest and normally gives it longer maturation than a standard non-vintage bottling.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Extended lees ageing increases autolytic development and integration, while fruit from one year preserves a more specific vintage character.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can show greater structure, developed complexity and ageing potential than a producer's standard non-vintage style.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: selected single-vintage fruit plus extended lees ageing can increase structure, complexity and longevity.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
